title: TalkOrigins Archive: Modernized Preview
description: A SciSiteForge proof-of-concept for poc1.talkorigins.org and the TalkOrigins Archive modernization line.

translation.system_prompt: You are a careful scientific translator. Preserve meaning, structure, citations, and technical terms. Return only the translation.

hero.title: A clearer front door for the TalkOrigins Archive.
hero.lede: The modernization work keeps the Archive's stable reference role while making it easier for a first-time reader to find explanations, claims, search tools, and sister Foundation resources.

feature_cards.title: New to the topic?
feature_cards.body: Start with readable explanations that separate the scientific issues from the rhetoric around them.

feature_cards.title: Looking for a specific claim?
feature_cards.body: The Index to Creationist Claims gives readers a direct path from a familiar claim to a response and references.

feature_cards.title: Searching across Foundation sites?
feature_cards.body: Foundation search keeps TalkOrigins, TalkDesign, Panda's Thumb, Panda's Thumb MT, and the claim index as distinct selectable corpora.

section_cards.title: Featured FAQ: Misconceptions
section_cards.body: A representative long-form Archive article demonstrates how legacy explanatory material can be presented in the modern theme.

section_cards.title: Featured Claim: CA100
section_cards.body: A representative Index to Creationist Claims entry shows the claim-centered path into evidence and rebuttal material.

bibliography_entries.title: TalkOrigins Archive
bibliography_entries.body: Stable explanatory articles and FAQs for recurring creation/evolution controversy topics.

bibliography_entries.title: Index to Creationist Claims
bibliography_entries.body: Claim-indexed rebuttal and reference material used as a cross-site search and notebook anchor.

bibliography_entries.title: Panda's Thumb MT Archive
bibliography_entries.body: Definitive corpus for the Movable Type-era Panda's Thumb archive, distinct from the broader scraped corpus.

notebooks.title: Evidence and Claims Reading Path
notebooks.summary: A guided path can connect stable Archive articles, Index to Creationist Claims entries, search results, and bibliography updates without hiding where each source came from.
notebooks.audience: interested lay readers, instructors, and site editors
goals: Move from a claim to the relevant evidence and archive context
Keep stable source material separate from dynamic commentary
Show provenance, citations, and review status as part of the reading experience

apps.title: Public search
apps.description: Search across Foundation corpora and the Index to Creationist Claims

roadmap.title: Replace the preview placeholders with real Archive pages
roadmap.body: Use the modernization theme on selected high-value FAQs and claim pages so readers can evaluate actual content, not just a shell.

roadmap.title: Promote corpus-aware Foundation search
roadmap.body: Make search visible from each Foundation-associated domain with Index and local-domain corpora selected by default.

roadmap.title: Connect support messaging to visible modernization work
roadmap.body: Use the proof-of-concept site to show that the Archive is being actively maintained while preserving the stable Archive and dynamic commentary split.

roadmap.title: Preserve legacy TalkOrigins URLs
roadmap.body: For this long-running Archive, English content should remain reachable at root paths such as /faqs/ and /indexcc/. Future language variants can live under language-code paths while the original English URLs continue to resolve.

roadmap.title: Add guided reading paths
roadmap.body: Generalize the notebook pattern into reader-friendly paths that help users move from questions, claims, and classroom needs to reliable Archive material.
